summary: On 2 June Ukraine reported a massive combined air strike involving 642 targets destroyed, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, with the main focus on Kyiv and attacks on Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Poltava. Separate attacks killed civilians in Dnipro (9 dead, including a child) and caused casualties in Kyiv (4 dead, 58 injured). Russia announced a ban on aviation fuel exports to 30 November, citing attacks on its oil facilities.
key_points: Massive combined air strike on 2 June destroyed 642 targets (40 rockets, 602 UAVs) mainly against Kyiv and also Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukraine recorded 4 civilian deaths and 58 injuries in a night‑long air raid on Kyiv, targeting residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
A cluster munition strike in Dnipro killed 9 people, including a three‑year‑old child, damaged 50 houses and destroyed 7 completely (Source: @UkraineNow/81614).
Russia banned aviation fuel exports effective 30 Nov, linking the decision to Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81592).
Russian forces used hyper‑velocity weapons on strategic industrial sites in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi, Sumy and fuel‑energy complexes (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian air defence reported over 600 UAVs and about 80 rockets launched overnight; most Tushok missiles were intercepted (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).
A reactive Shahed drone was observed over the Kyiv sea heading toward Vyshorod (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67457).

sections.executive_summary: Massive combined air strike on 2 June destroyed 642 targets, focusing on Kyiv and striking multiple eastern regions (Source: @kpszsu/63655). Separate attacks caused civilian casualties in Dnipro and Kyiv (Sources: @UkraineNow/81614, @UkraineNow/81607). Russia responded with an aviation‑fuel export ban and announced use of hyper‑velocity weapons against Ukrainian infrastructure (Sources: @UkraineNow/81592, @UkraineNow/81610).

points: Ukrainian air defence intercepted over 600 UAVs and roughly 80 rockets during the night of 1‑2 June, reporting that most Tushok missiles were shot down (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).
In Dnipro, a cluster munition strike killed nine civilians, including a three‑year‑old child, damaged 50 houses and destroyed seven completely (Source: @UkraineNow/81614).
Kyiv suffered a night‑long air raid with four civilian deaths and 58 injuries, the targets being residential buildings and other civil infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed drone over the Kyiv sea heading toward Vyshorod, indicating continued low‑altitude drone threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67457).

Ukrainian air units landed three Tu‑95MS bombers at Olenya airfield and three Tu‑160 strategic bombers en route to the Ukrainianka airfield, showing continued strategic bomber operations (Source: @eRadarrua/94896).
Russia announced a ban on aviation fuel exports effective 30 Nov, citing Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil facilities as the reason (Source: @UkraineNow/81592).

The Russian Ministry of Defence reported the use of high‑precision, long‑range weapons including hypersonic missiles against Ukrainian defence‑industrial sites in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi, Sumy and fuel‑energy complexes (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).

Russian forces launched a massive combined strike on 2 June employing 40 rockets (including Iskander‑M, Kh‑101, Kalibr, and 3M22 Circon) and 656 attack UAVs from multiple launch areas such as Crimea, Kursk and Bryansk (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Russian air defence claimed to have shot down 148 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions during the night (Source: @tass_agency/378546).
The 2 June combined strike hit eastern regions including Kharkiv, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia and Poltava, with confirmed impacts on industrial and civilian targets (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian reports indicated a Shahed drone over the Kyiv sea heading toward Vyshorod, a potential vector for attacks on eastern supply routes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67457).
Russian hyper‑velocity weapon use targeted the defence‑industrial complex in Kharkiv and Dnipro, aiming at critical manufacturing facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted a large number of UAVs and rockets during the night, reducing the effectiveness of the eastern barrage (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).

Ukrainian reports listed multiple UAV movements in northern oblasts: Zhytomyr to Ovrych, Kyiv region near Slavutych, and Rivne towards Rokytne (Sources: @eRadarrua/94928, @eRadarrua/94921, @kpszsu/63676).
Shahed drones were observed over Chernihiv region near Slavutych, indicating continued pressure on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/94889).
Russian missile launches from Crimea and Kursk targeted northern Ukrainian airspace, part of the 2 June combined strike (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian air defence reported successful interceptions of UAVs and rockets over northern territories during the night (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).
Ukrainian sources reported UAVs from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region, vectoring southward (Source: @kpszsu/63667).
Russian hyper‑velocity weapons were used against fuel‑energy complexes in southern oblasts, including Khmelnytskyi and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
A Shahed drone was observed over the Kyiv sea heading toward Vyshorod, suggesting potential southern maritime approaches (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67457).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted numerous UAVs and rockets during the night, protecting southern infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).
On 2 June, Ukraine reported 602 enemy UAVs and 40 rockets destroyed, with the main axis of attack directed at Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian air defence shot down over 600 UAVs and about 80 rockets overnight, including most Tushok missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).
Russian forces employed a mix of ballistic (Iskander‑M), cruise (Kh‑101, Kalibr) and anti‑ship (Circon) missiles in the combined strike (Source: @kpszsu/63655).

Three Tu‑95MS bombers landed at Olenya airfield after missile launches, and three Tu‑160 strategic bombers are en route to Ukrainianka, indicating continued strategic bomber activity (Source: @eRadarrua/94896).

key_developments: Massive combined air strike on 2 June destroyed 642 targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, with Kyiv as the primary target and secondary strikes on Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted over 600 UAVs and about 80 rockets overnight, reporting most Tushok missiles were shot down (Source: @eRadarrua/94910).
A cluster munition attack in Dnipro killed nine civilians, including a three‑year‑old child, and damaged 50 houses (Source: @UkraineNow/81614).
Night‑long air raid on Kyiv resulted in four civilian deaths and 58 injuries, targeting residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Russia imposed an aviation‑fuel export ban effective 30 Nov, citing Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81592).
Russian Ministry of Defence announced use of hypersonic and long‑range precision weapons against Ukrainian defence‑industrial sites across multiple regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81610).
Three Tu‑95MS bombers landed at Olenya airfield and three Tu‑160 strategic bombers are heading to Ukrainianka after missile launches (Source: @eRadarrua/94896).
sections.threat_assessment: The coordinated 2 June air strike demonstrates Russia's capability to launch large‑scale, multi‑vector attacks combining ballistic, cruise and UAV assets, with a focus on Kyiv and key eastern industrial hubs. Ukrainian air defence showed resilience, intercepting the majority of incoming UAVs and rockets, but civilian casualties remain high, especially from cluster munitions. The Russian export ban on aviation fuel may strain Ukrainian air operations, while the deployment of hypersonic weapons signals an escalation in strike precision and reach.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's northern and southern fronts reported increased UAV activity, prompting heightened radar monitoring and rapid interception efforts. Eastern regions, particularly Kharkiv and Dnipro, faced intensified missile strikes on industrial facilities, leading to localized infrastructure disruptions. Russian diplomatic moves, such as the fuel export ban, aim to pressure Ukraine's logistical capabilities while reinforcing Russia's strategic messaging.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch sites in Crimea, Kursk and Bryansk is critical, as they supplied the majority of the 656 attack drones. The continued use of Shahed drones over the Kyiv sea suggests a maritime infiltration route that requires naval and coastal air defence coordination. The reported success in intercepting Tushok missiles indicates effective Ukrainian SAM performance, but the sheer volume of attacks underscores the need for sustained air defence resupply.
